name: Texas Beef Barbecue
recipeIngredient: 1 medium Fresh Or Canned Green Chile
recipeInstructions:
1. When the fire has burned down to hot coals, spread them to provide moderate heat and place a drip pan in front of the coals in the center.
2. Place the brisket, fat side up, over the drip pan. Cover the grill and adjust the dampers to maintain slow steady heat.
3. In a saucepan, combine all other ingredients and simmer for 10 minutes.
4. After 1 hour, baste the meat lightly with the sauce and turn as needed to cook evenly.
5. Replenish the fire as needed, but do not pile the coals; the brisket should cook slowly.
6. Cook until the meat is tender (about 4-5 hours total).
7. Sprinkle 2 to 3 handfuls of soaked hickory chips over the coals. Cover the grill or enclose the top of the meat in a sheet of foil, tucked around the bottom edges of the meat.
8. Let the hickory smoke the meat for 10 to 15 minutes.
9. Remove the cover and brush the meat with the sauce.
10. Place the meat on a platter and slice. Serve on split and buttered sandwich buns. Leftover sandwiches can be frozen and reheated in a microwave or conventional oven, or the shredded meat and sauce can be frozen together for later use.
